title: Guyana Part 2 - Boarder town, Truck drive accross the jungle

transcript.text: Yes, I got a beer there. My friend, this guy I was hanging out with, he, uh, I guess he lived, he was a tour guide in Manaus, in the jungle, and he was coming back because his dad died, and he had to, like, square stuff up with his family, and, because he was inheriting a plot of land, I guess, in that town where his dad lived, but his brother was in Georgetown, so he was going to go visit him.

And, uh, so, we went and were waiting for the big truck to take us. I could have taken a, it would have been a lot more luxurious to just take a Land Rover and get in the back seat of it, but, uh, but this guy talked me into, um, doing the Bedford, these huge Bedford trucks, they're, like, the tires are, like, four feet high, and, uh, and they're huge, they're these huge, like, pickup type trucks,

But they're way bigger. They're, like, as big as semis, and, uh, and I guess they're made by England, and they're from World War II, and, uh, it's, like, the only truck that can actually drive across that country, because it's just, it goes across the jungle,

And, uh, and so it's really wet and rainy and muddy all the time, and so these Bedfords, because their tires are so big, like, their wheelbase is, like, that high, so they drive, and they make these, uh, holes with their tires, they're literally that deep
And that wide, so any other car that comes would have, has to just completely avoid the tracks that they make, and they go in there, and I guess they're so heavy,
That even if the wheelbase, or the, you know, the hole is really deep, they can just, they just plow through it and make it, but, uh,
We were waiting there for, like, a half day, and I guess there was these trees that you shake, and these nuts come off, so we made a fire, and then you, uh, throw the,
Throw the nuts on this, uh, little grate, and they get roast, and then you break them up and eat them, it's kind of cool,
And then the car came, the truck came, and he's, like, oh, no, someone stole all of my money,
Oh, no, I, it must have happened when I wasn't looking, you're gonna have to pay for me,
Don't worry, though, I'll pay you, because my brother is in Georgetown, um, and his cousin's, like, oh, no,
Oh, duppies, oh, man, so anyway, I, I paid for him to go, and, um, anyways, yeah, I guess I wouldn't, if, if, if,
Lesson from this is if you ever meet somebody, and one of these poor Catanas is, like, wants to be your
Buddy and everything, it's probably for a reason, so just don't hang out with him, only hang out with
People who aren't in your face wanting to be your buddy, or just other tourists, but, because that
Happened to me also, and then Honduras, I loaned this Honduran guy 150 bucks, I know that's making
Me look like a complete idiot, but I'm just gullible, what can I say, and he was gonna come
Back and pay me back, but he never did, so I found him in Rotten, he didn't even, like, recognize me,
And he couldn't pay me back, obviously, but, anyways, um, um, so we get in this Bedford thing,
And there's, like, all these people, well, not all these people, there's maybe a half dozen people
Who got in the Bedford, because the Bedford was mainly just a, just a loader truck, like,
Take, carrying, uh, carrying, uh, what do you call it, just gear, stuff, cargo, so all the people
Were hanging out on the top of this thing, on top of all the cargo, way, way, way, way up
Off the ground, like, 15 feet up off the ground, or something like that, or 10 feet up off the
Ground, but it was cool, because you got to feel the wind going through your hair, and you just hold
On to one of the straps that holds all your stuff down, and, uh, and, uh, driving down the road, and
They were going really fast, too, I felt like we were driving down the outback of Australia, um, there
Was a woman there, kind of an older woman, with, like, a three-year-old kid, and one time we hit a
Bump, and he hit his head on the metal bar really hard, and started crying, and she's, like,
Stop your crying, boy, but, um, and then his cousin, my buddy, and his cousin was interesting, he was a,
Uh, he was a endangered bird smuggler, I think, because he, he was holding these birds, and said
He could sell them for, like, thousands of dollars each, and so, um, but he said he, yeah, he'd go way
Up in the jungle, and you could see the mountains down south of the town we were in, they're really
High, and they're, like, the only mountains in the jungle, like, their highest mountains in the Amazon
Jungle, they were kind of, like, in the border between, um, Guyana and Brazil, and he'd go up in
There and trap these birds, and then bring them back to Georgetown and sell them to, like, get exported
Someplace to some, like, first world countries where people collect those kind of stuff, but, uh, he had
Some interesting stories, he said he knew, like, he had met, like, the Indians, and the Indians who live out
There, or, like, he said, he said he was in a canoe once, and he was, uh, out there in the jungle in the
Middle of nowhere, and he saw this, like, monster pop up, and was, like, a giant head, like, this big
Eyes, like, this big, just look at him, he didn't even know what it was, and it was huge, and then it
Floated away, but he said that, like, the anacondas out there are gigantic, and they, uh, they, they eat
Deer, but they eat the deer by hypnotizing the deer, they slither up to the deer, and they stare right
In the deer's face, and the deer just, just stares at it, and doesn't run, and then it comes up and
Captures the deer, but, uh, the Indians out there don't have to worry about the anacondas at all,
Because they have these, uh, because they hypnotize the anacondas, and, uh, and they, uh, they also have
These big anaconda teeth around their chest, which I guess protects them from the anacondas,
Um, but he was really, like, worried about keep watching his, his, his, his, uh, his birds, he kept
The cage totally covered in, uh, in, like, blankets, and whenever the birds would start flapping and
Stuff, he'd kind of freak out and make sure that the, there was no air getting in, if the blankets
Were covered, so I guess his birds, sometimes the birds would die or something, a fright on what would
Drive down, but, uh, so, yeah, we drove by this plane part that was kind of like Australia, and then we got
To the jungle, or we're going through the jungle, and, uh, yeah, with these huge divots, and, and the
Truck started to go really slowly at this point, like, maybe 10 or 15 miles an hour, or slower, maybe,
Yeah, 15 miles an hour, so, yeah, it took, like, 24 hours of constant driving just to get across this
Country, which was maybe 500 miles long or something, and, yeah, right through the middle of
The jungle, and, um, we got to this river, and, uh, it's the only big river, but there's no bridge on
This river, it's a really poor country, so they had to pay this big, like, these huge, like, boats that
Were big enough to carry, they were just these platform boats that were big enough to carry, like, the
Bedford, and then a couple other pickups, um, across the river, which was maybe a couple hundred feet
Wide, and all they had to power it was, like, a normal rotor, motorboat, uh, motor, so they go across, and
Then, um, and then, uh, I had, for some reason, they waited, like, an hour or something before they started
Driving again, I guess the drivers were having lunch or something, so I went up and got lunch at this lady
Who was, who was, who was, I guess, in charge of some way station restaurant who lived way up, just in the
Jungle, and she fixed me a, uh, sandwich or something, but she was really gruff, like, uh, um, just kind of,
Like, she kept talking, but kind of, there was this black guy there who had this big afro, and he was missing
All these fingers, and I guess he was her worker, but he didn't live with her or something, because
She's just like, yeah, don't be stealing any more of my, my bread to each other, and he didn't talk at
All, he looked like he was, like, retarded or something, but, uh, it was, uh, really basic
Situation she had, just a toothpick house, and cardboard toothpick cordigated iron house, and the,
Her eating area was just outside a table, you know, maybe with a, you know, a little thing on here
Or something on top, then we get back on the truck, and we're driving down to Bedford, and I, uh,
There were less people on it this time, so I think I was the only guy on the back,
So my buddy sat in the front with some other guy, and I was on the top, um, but I would have to, uh,
But since we're driving through the jungle, oh, whoops, I am out of time again, oh, take,
